title: 13 FX Notes Settlement and Trace Request
body_text:
13	FX Notes Settlement and Trace Request
13.1	FX Notes Settlement for BC and Ontario Financial Institutions
FX Notes transactions are settled in a net settlement amount to your financial institutionâs account. Settlement is in Canadian dollars and is posted by branch or to a designated transit number (e.g., head office).
Central 1 posts a net settlement amount to your financial institutionâs Canadian dollar current account based on wholesale value, including fees. The transaction code FC is used. Since settlement is netted, the amount posted to your account could be a credit or a debit.
Settlement is posted the business day after Central 1 receives the settlement file from Globex 2000 and is based on the wholesale value of FX Notes transactions (i.e., without your financial institutionâs spread or fees). The transaction code is FC.
Note:	Non-business-day transactions (e.g., Saturday) are processed on the next business day.
Transactions are included in the following reports:
AIBC â Central 1 Account Activity and Balances (BC financial institutions), and
AIOC â Central 1 Account Activity and Balances (Ontario financial institutions). See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Account Activity and Balances (AIBC/AIOU)
Download these reports from Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. The AIBC report (BC financial institutions) is in the root directory. The AIOC report (Ontario financial institutions) is available in the ONDL file in the root directory. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Postings can also be viewed on the Central 1 Banking System. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
13.2	FX Notes Settlement for Non-Member Financial Institutions
Settlement is netted so the amount sent to your financial institution via AFT could be a credit or a debit. See the following table for details.

13.3	Reconciliation of FX Notes Currency Orders
Your financial institutionâs revenues for FX Notes currency orders are displayed on the Profitability report. Only Manager and Auditor roles can access these reports.
The Profitability report can be used to reconcile against your financial institutionâs account settlement. The report includes the wholesale and customer transaction details to determine the profit on each transaction. The posting for the current dayâs transactions appears on the next business dayâs report.
To reconcile currency transactions, generate a Profitability report as described in Section 14, FX Notes Reports. Download Central 1âs AIBC (in BC) or AIOC (in Ontario) report to reconcile the totals that have an FC transaction code for the applicable dates. For non-member financial institutions, compare the Profitability report to the report that you receive from your Provincial Central.
13.4	Settlement to Your Customerâs Account
Debit or credit your customerâs account for the total transaction at the time of the transaction. Follow your internal procedures.
END
